compiled_state.title: Agents can decrease review tier to circumvent the review process
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: During the review process for eb00-efd0, the standard-tier reviewer (sonnet) scored correctness at 3 with an important finding. Per REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md, the autonomous resolution loop should escalate the tier upward on repeated failures (light->standard->deep). However, there is no enforcement preventing an agent from re-running the classifier on a smaller post-fix diff to get a lower tier score, or from substituting a lighter agent. The TIER IMMUTABILITY rule in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md states the tier is locked once set, but this is guidance only — there is no code-level enforcement. An agent rationalizing around the tier lock (e.g., 'the diff is now smaller so light tier is appropriate') could bypass the review system. Need: a code-level enforcement mechanism that prevents tier downgrades during a review session, similar to how record-review.sh validates diff hashes.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral (dual-signal: ticket describes agent behavior circumvention + affected files are in hooks/scripts). Score: N/A (llm-behavioral path).
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned (high confidence). TIER IMMUTABILITY is documented in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md but zero code enforcement exists. Investigation confirmed: record-review.sh, pre-commit-review-gate.sh, and write-reviewer-findings.sh contain no tier validation.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Reclassified: this is a feature request (adding new enforcement code), not a bug fix. Existing TIER IMMUTABILITY rule in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md is guidance-only by design — adding code enforcement is new capability. Routing to /dso:brainstorm.
comments.author: Test
